gist_memory.paper_problem_memory: Classical directed evolution is labor- and time-intensive due to multiple manual steps (DNA extraction, in vitro mutagenesis, transformation, selection) and limited screening throughput. The goal is to develop an ultrahigh-throughput in vivo continuous directed evolution system with thermosensitive inducible tunability to rapidly engineer enzymes and biosynthetic pathways.

section_gists.heading: Abstract
section_gists.gist: Presents an ultrahigh-throughput in vivo continuous directed evolution system using error-prone DNA Pol I controlled by engineered thermosensitive repressor cI857 and a temperature-sensitive MutS mutant. Achieved ~600-fold increase in targeted mutation rate. Applied to evolve β-lactamase, α-amylase (48.3% activity improvement via microfluidic droplet screening), and resveratrol biosynthetic pathway (1.7-fold higher production via FACS).
process_signals: System integrates thermal-responsive targeted mutagenesis with ultrahigh-throughput screening.
Demonstrated on model enzyme (β-lactamase) and applied to industrial enzyme (α-amylase) and pathway (resveratrol).
methods: Error-prone DNA Pol I expression modulated by cI857
Genomic MutS mutant with temperature-sensitive defect

section_gists.heading: Background
section_gists.gist: Classical directed evolution is limited by manual, labor-intensive cycles. In vivo continuous evolution systems (e.g., PACE, OrthoRep) reduce human intervention but often couple protein activity to cell growth/survival, which is not always possible. Ultrahigh-throughput screening (FACS, droplet microfluidics) can link genotype to phenotype for non-selectable traits. This study aims to develop a temperature-controlled in vivo evolution system using DNA Pol I and MutS mutant, combined with ultrahigh-throughput screening.
process_signals: Need for spatiotemporal regulation of mutagenesis to avoid off-target effects.
Combining in vivo mutagenesis with ultrahigh-throughput screening to evolve enzymes and pathways without growth coupling.
methods: Temperature-sensitive MutS for transient MMR deficiency
FACS and droplet microfluidics for screening
failures_or_limits: Existing in vivo systems often require growth/survival coupling, limiting applicability.

section_gists.heading: Directed evolution of the highly thermosensitive repressor cl857
section_gists.gist: To achieve tight regulation of Pol I*, the λ PR-cI857 system was evolved for lower leakage at 30°C and higher expression at 37°C. A cI857 mutant library was screened via two rounds of FACS: first at 37°C for high fluorescence, then at 30°C for low fluorescence. An evolved mutant M1 (cI857* with ΔT57, A400T, T418A) showed similar repression at 30°C but 134.8-fold induction at 37°C vs 30°C, and ~13.8-fold higher induction than wild-type at 37°C.
process_signals: Initial cI857 had insufficient dynamic range for tight control.
Two-step FACS strategy effectively enriched mutants with desired thermal response.
M1 mutant significantly improved ON/OFF ratio.
methods: Fluorescence assay in 96-well plates and shake flasks

section_gists.heading: Temperature-controlled in vivo evolution based on DNA Pol I in E. coli
section_gists.gist: A two-plasmid system was built: pSC101 (low-copy) with pol I* under PR-cI857* control, and pET28a (ColE1 ori) with target gene. Reversion assays using premature TAA in β-lactamase or aadA showed tightly regulated mutagenesis: low reversion at 30°C, high at 37°C. Mutation rate decreased with distance from ori. cI857* gave better regulation than cI857 on low-copy plasmid.
process_signals: Choice of Pol I* (D424A I709N A759R) based on prior work.
Two-plasmid design separates mutator and target to avoid self-mutation.
Reversion assay quantifies targeted mutation rate.
cI857* improved dynamic range on low-copy plasmid.
methods: Reversion assay with TAA stop codon in antibiotic resistance genes
metrics: Reversion frequency at 500 bp: ~72 (Pol I*, 37°C) vs ~5 (no Pol I)
Reversion rate decreases with distance from ori
failures_or_limits: Mutation rate limited by Pol I* activity and distance from ori.

section_gists.heading: Synergistic action with MutS to accelerate the evolution rate
section_gists.gist: To increase mutation rate, a temperature-sensitive MutS mutant (MutS60, A134V) was introduced into the host genome. At 43°C, MutS60 with Pol I* gave optimal plasmid mutation rate (~638-fold higher than WT with Pol I at 250 bp). Mutation rate was ~6.77×10⁻⁸ per nucleotide per generation, ~600-fold above background. Genomic mutation rate increased ~70-fold. Plasmid extraction and retransformation recommended to avoid off-target genomic mutations.
process_signals: Initial mutation rate with Pol I* alone may have been insufficient for efficient evolution.
MutS60 provides transient MMR deficiency at elevated temperature.
Temperature optimization (43°C) balances mutation rate and cell growth.
Genomic mutation rate increase is a concern; plasmid retransformation mitigates this.
methods: Rifampicin-resistance assay for genomic mutation rate
metrics: ~638-fold increase in plasmid reversion rate at 250 bp
Mutation rate: 6.77×10⁻⁸ per nt per generation
failures_or_limits: Off-target genomic mutagenesis requires plasmid retransformation between rounds.

section_gists.heading: Mutational spectrum analysis
section_gists.gist: Sequencing of reversion mutants revealed mutational bias. With Pol I* alone, TAA→TTA (61%) predominated. With MutS60, TAA→CAA (85%) became dominant, indicating a shift in spectrum due to MMR deficiency. This may affect the types of accessible mutations.
process_signals: Mutational spectrum is biased and changes with MutS60.
Spectrum knowledge is important for library design and understanding evolutionary outcomes.
methods: Sanger sequencing of reverted β-lactamase and aadA genes
metrics: Pol I* alone: TAA→TTA 61%, TCA 18%, CAA 14%
MutS60 + Pol I*: TAA→CAA 85%, AAA 11%, TAC 4%
failures_or_limits: Strong mutational bias may limit sequence space exploration.

section_gists.heading: Microfluidic droplet screening-assisted in vivo evolution of α-amylase
section_gists.gist: Applied system to evolve α-amylase (BLA) displayed on E. coli surface. After 25 rounds of mutagenesis-enrichment, growth rate in enrichment medium increased. Droplet microfluidic screening of ~3,000,000 droplets sorted top 0.1% fluorescent droplets. 72% of sorted cells had >20% increased activity. A mutant N473Y showed 48.3% activity improvement, attributed to modestly increased specific activity and stability.
process_signals: Enrichment rounds prior to screening enriched for improved variants.
Droplet screening enabled ultrahigh-throughput (3M droplets).
Identified single beneficial mutation (N473Y) with significant improvement.
methods: Surface display of BLA via Lpp-OmpA fusion
Droplet microfluidics with DQ starch substrate
metrics: 72% of sorted cells had >20% activity increase

section_gists.heading: In vivo evolution of the resveratrol biosynthesis pathway in engineered E. coli
section_gists.gist: Applied system to resveratrol pathway (4CL and STS). Developed TtgR-based biosensor specific for resveratrol. After 3 rounds of mutagenesis-FACS, a mutant with 1.7-fold higher titer was isolated. Mutations were in the pETDuet backbone (promoter/UTR) rather than enzymes, leading to increased mRNA levels. This highlights that pathway evolution can improve expression rather than enzyme activity.
process_signals: Biosensor specificity was validated (low response to p-coumaric acid).
Multiple rounds of mutagenesis and FACS enrichment.
Unexpectedly, beneficial mutations occurred in plasmid backbone, not enzymes.
Demonstrates system can evolve pathway performance via expression optimization.
methods: 3 rounds of mutagenesis and FACS sorting
metrics: Increased mRNA levels of pathway enzymes
failures_or_limits: Mutations not in enzymes but in expression elements; may not be generalizable.

section_gists.heading: Discussion
section_gists.gist: Dynamic regulation via temperature shift is advantageous for controlling mutagenesis. However, Pol I* may cause off-target genomic mutations, exacerbated by MutS60. Mutation rate (6.77×10⁻⁸/bp/generation) is 3-4 orders of magnitude lower than advanced systems, limiting sequence exploration. Ultrahigh-throughput screening (FACS, microfluidics) overcomes the challenge of identifying rare beneficial variants. The system is compared to recent similar work by Rosenthal et al.
process_signals: Acknowledges off-target genomic mutagenesis as a key limitation.
Low mutation rate compared to other systems (e.g., OrthoRep, PACE) is a drawback.
Ultrahigh-throughput screening is essential to find rare improved variants.
Plasmid retransformation is necessary to reset genomic background.
failures_or_limits: Need for plasmid retransformation between rounds

section_gists.heading: Conclusions
section_gists.gist: Developed a temperature-controlled in vivo continuous evolution system with >600-fold increased targeted mutation rate. Combined with ultrahigh-throughput screening, achieved 48.3% α-amylase activity improvement and 1.7-fold resveratrol production increase. Demonstrates promise for protein engineering and cell factory construction.

section_gists.heading: Availability of data and materials
section_gists.gist: Data available in article and supplementary materials.

global_hypotheses_about_storyline: Initial hypothesis: Temperature-controlled expression of error-prone Pol I can enable in vivo targeted mutagenesis of plasmids.
Evolved cI857* improves dynamic range, enabling tighter control.
Adding MutS60 increases mutation rate by transiently disabling MMR at high temperature.
Combining with ultrahigh-throughput screening allows evolution of non-selectable traits (enzyme activity, pathway flux).
The system can be applied to both single enzymes and multi-enzyme pathways.
uncertainties_to_resolve: Exact number of rounds and selection pressures for α-amylase evolution beyond 25 enrichment rounds.
Detailed characterization of off-target genomic mutations and their impact on cell fitness.
Whether the low mutation rate can be improved without increasing genomic instability.
Generalizability of the biosensor approach to other pathways.
Long-term stability of evolved strains without continuous selection.

events.summary: Analysis of resveratrol pathway evolution reveals that beneficial mutations occurred in the plasmid backbone (ColE1 ori C148T and Δ5526-6717 deletion) rather than in the enzyme genes, leading to increased mRNA levels and resveratrol production.

The in vivo mutagenesis system has a low mutation rate (6.77×10⁻⁸ per base per generation), which is 3–4 orders of magnitude lower than advanced tools, limiting sequence space exploration and evolution speed.
Off-target genomic mutations occur due to Pol I* involvement in genome replication and defective mismatch repair, potentially affecting cell survival and screening.

findings.problem: Step 5 claims to measure optimal mutation rate at 43°C with Pol I* and MutS60, but the evidence provided (EV_C002_E04_02, EV_C002_E04_04) does not explicitly mention 43°C or the specific mutation rate 6.77×10⁻⁸ per nt per generation. The step also lacks evidence for the ~70-fold genomic mutation rate increase. The decision to use 43°C is unsupported by the cited evidence.
findings.revision_instruction: Add evidence that explicitly states the temperature (43°C) and the mutation rate (6.77×10⁻⁸ per nt per generation) and the ~70-fold genomic increase, or adjust the step to reflect only what the evidence supports.

findings.problem: Step 2 mentions 134.8-fold induction and 13.8-fold higher than WT at 37°C, but the provided evidence (EV_AUTO_001, EV_AUTO_022) does not contain these numbers. EV_AUTO_022 is a figure caption without quantitative data.
findings.revision_instruction: Include evidence that explicitly states the 134.8-fold induction and 13.8-fold higher values, or remove these numbers from the step if not supported.

findings.problem: Step 7 claims 72% of sorted cells showed >20% increased activity, but the evidence EV_C002_E07_02 is not included in the step's evidence list. The step cites EV_AUTO_034 which mentions 1% sorting threshold, conflicting with the 0.1% threshold in the step.
findings.revision_instruction: Add EV_C002_E07_02 to the step's evidence and resolve the discrepancy between 0.1% and 1% sorting thresholds, or clarify which threshold was used.

findings.problem: Step 11 states 1.7-fold higher resveratrol production, but this number is not directly supported by the cited evidence. The evidence mentions 4-fold mRNA increase and 2.9-fold mRNA level, but not the 1.7-fold production increase.
findings.revision_instruction: Add evidence that explicitly states the 1.7-fold increase in resveratrol production, or adjust the step to reflect only the mRNA level increases.

findings.problem: Step 4 includes EV_AUTO_017 (TtgR biosensor) which is unrelated to the reversion assay. This evidence is misplaced and does not support the step.
findings.revision_instruction: Remove EV_AUTO_017 from step 4 and ensure all evidence is relevant to the reversion assay.

findings.problem: Step 6 includes EV_AUTO_001 (cI857* mutations) and EV_C005_E03_03 (droplet incubation) which are not directly relevant to the α-amylase enrichment experiment. These may be extraneous.
findings.revision_instruction: Remove irrelevant evidence from step 6 and keep only evidence directly supporting the α-amylase evolution and growth improvement.

findings.problem: Step 12 bundles three limitations (K15, K16, K18) into one step, but the evidence for K15 (EV_C004_E02_02) does not mention the specific mutation rate 6.77×10⁻⁸. The step also lacks evidence for the off-target genomic mutation rate.
findings.revision_instruction: Split the limitations into separate steps or ensure each limitation has its own supporting evidence. Add evidence that explicitly states the low mutation rate value if it is to be included.

must_keep: The overall trajectory from problem to hypothesis to system design to validation is logically sound.

Key events like cI857* evolution, two-plasmid system, reversion assay, MutS60 addition, α-amylase evolution, droplet screening, N473Y validation, biosensor development, and resveratrol pathway evolution are all present.
The limitations are correctly identified and placed at the end.
self_critic.summary: The candidate trajectory covers all required mainline events and follows the correct order. However, several steps lack direct evidence for specific numbers (e.g., 134.8-fold induction, 43°C, 6.77×10⁻⁸ mutation rate, 1.7-fold production increase) and include irrelevant evidence. The 72% activity increase claim is missing its evidence, and there is a discrepancy in sorting thresholds. These issues reduce the reliability of the trajectory and should be corrected by adding or adjusting evidence.

initial_request.research_problem: Classical directed evolution is labor- and time-intensive due to manual steps and limited screening throughput.

constraints: Must enable continuous in vivo evolution
quantifiable_goals: Achieve >20% activity improvement in target enzymes

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Classical directed evolution is labor- and time-intensive due to manual steps and limited screening throughput.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need for an ultrahigh-throughput in vivo continuous directed evolution system with thermosensitive inducible tunability.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Temperature-controlled expression of error-prone Pol I can enable in vivo targeted mutagenesis of plasmids.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Develop an in vivo continuous evolution system based on error-prone DNA polymerase I (Pol I*) under control of a thermosensitive repressor.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Hypothesis formulated that thermosensitive repressor cI857 can regulate Pol I* expression.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Need to evolve a tighter repressor to achieve better regulation.

an ultrahigh throughput in vivo continuous directed evolution system with thermosensitive inducible tunability, which is based on error-prone DNA polymerase expression modulated by engineered thermal-responsive repressor cl857

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Wild-type cI857 repressor provides some temperature sensitivity but may not be tight enough.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need tighter regulation of Pol I* expression to minimize background mutagenesis at low temperature.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Evolving cI857 can yield a mutant with higher induction ratio.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Evolve cI857 to obtain cI857* with mutations ΔT57, A400T, T418A.
evolution_trajectory.observation: cI857* achieved tighter regulation; specific induction fold values not directly quoted in provided evidence.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Use cI857* to construct a two-plasmid system.

evidence.quote_or_span: Compared with wild-type cI857, the cI857 mutant (cI857\*) from the M1 strain has three changes in the DNA sequence, including two substitutions and one deletion ( $\Delta$ T57, A400T and T418A).

evolution_trajectory.state_before: cI857* provides tight regulation, but a system architecture is needed.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to separate mutagenesis machinery from target gene to avoid interference.

evolution_trajectory.observation: Two-plasmid system built.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Validate mutagenesis regulation with reversion assay.

an ultrahigh throughput in vivo continuous directed evolution system with thermosensitive inducible tunability, which is based on error-prone DNA polymerase expression modulated by engineered thermal-responsive repressor cl857

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Two-plasmid system constructed but mutagenesis regulation unverified.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to confirm temperature-dependent mutagenesis and distance effect from ori.

evolution_trajectory.observation: Low reversion at 30°C, high at 37°C; mutation rate decreased with distance from ori; 41 times higher reversion rate at 250 bp.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Mutation rate still insufficient; consider disabling MMR.

evidence.quote_or_span: the distance from ori impacted the mutation rate

TtgR–P $_{ttgABC}$ system, which is responsible for multidrug recognition and especially efficient in responding to different flavonoids, is a native transcription regulatory system from Pseudomonas putida DOT–T1E [41, 42].

evolution_trajectory.state_before: MutS60 strain constructed but combined effect with Pol I* unknown.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to determine optimal temperature and mutation rate with both Pol I* and MutS60.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Higher temperature will increase mutation rate due to Pol I* activity and MutS60 inactivation.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Measure reversion rate at various temperatures with Pol I* and MutS60.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Optimal plasmid mutation rate at 43°C: ~638-fold higher than wild-type with Pol I at 250 bp; mutation rate 6.77×10⁻⁸ per nt per generation; ~70-fold genomic mutation rate increase.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Characterize mutational spectrum and apply to enzyme evolution.

evidence.quote_or_span: approximately 638 times that of the wild-type strain with Pol I at 250 bp
the mutation rate of the E. coli genome was increased by about 70-fold during mutagenesis

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Mutagenesis system characterized, ready for enzyme evolution.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to demonstrate system can improve enzyme activity.

evolution_trajectory.decision: Evolve BLA (α-amylase from Bacillus licheniformis) displayed on E. coli surface; perform 25 rounds of mutagenesis-enrichment.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Growth rate in enrichment medium increased with passages; after 1 round, OD600 reached 1.5 at 30 h.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Screen enriched library with droplet microfluidics.

evidence.quote_or_span: as the number of consecutive passages increased, the growth rate in enrichment medium showed a gradual increasing trend

b Growth of the experimental strain and the control strains cultured for 30 h in liquid enrichment medium containing 2% starch as the sole carbon source after mutagenesis, and the initial $OD_{600}$ was unified to 0.2.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Enriched α-amylase library obtained, but need high-throughput screening.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to identify individual improved variants from large library.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Droplet microfluidic FACS can sort top variants based on fluorescence.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Encapsulate cells in droplets with fluorogenic substrate, sort top 0.1% fluorescent droplets from ~3,000,000 droplets.
evolution_trajectory.observation: 0.1% of droplets sorted; 72% of sorted cells showed >20% increased activity compared to wild-type.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Identify and validate top mutant.

evidence.quote_or_span: 0.1% of droplets with fluorescence signals exceeding the set threshold were sorted from approximately 3,000,000 droplets
Seventy-two percent of sorted cells showed over 20% increased activity compared with the wild-type stain
Single cell droplets were then collected and incubated in a 1 mL syringe at 25 or 30 °C.
1% of droplets with fluorescence signals exceeding the set threshold were sorted from approximately 3,000,000 droplets in a short time.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Sorted α-amylase variants show improved fluorescence, but specific mutations and activity need confirmation.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to identify the mutation responsible and quantify activity improvement.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: The N473Y mutation is responsible for activity increase.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Sequence sorted clones, retransform N473Y mutant, and measure fermentation activity.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Retransformed strain carrying mutant BLA (N473Y) showed 48.3% activity increase; attributed to modestly increased specific activity and stability.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Apply system to biosynthetic pathway evolution.

evidence.quote_or_span: the fermentation activity of retransformed strain carrying mutant BLA (N473Y) increased by 48.3%
the combined effects of modestly increased specific enzyme activity and stability may contribute to the overall improvement
med strain carrying mutant BLA (N473Y) increased by 48.3%, due to a nucleotide change at 217 bp downstream of ColE1 ori
the combined effects of modestly increased specific enzyme activity and stability may contribute to the overall improvement in enzymatic activity

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Mutagenesis system proven for single enzyme, but pathway evolution requires product-specific screening.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need a biosensor to couple resveratrol production to fluorescence for FACS.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: TtgR-based biosensor can specifically detect resveratrol and drive mCherry expression.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Develop TtgR-PttgABC biosensor with mCherry reporter.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Biosensor showed excellent specificity for resveratrol over p-coumaric acid; fluorescence correlated with resveratrol titer.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Use biosensor for FACS-based evolution of resveratrol pathway.

evidence.quote_or_span: To realize the in vivo evolution of these key enzymes, a biosensor-based FACS method was established
The induction was much lower in response to p-coumaric acid than to resveratrol, which demonstrated the excellent specificity of this sensor for resveratrol
The fluorescence intensity was positively correlated with the titer of resveratrol, which exhibited that the biosensor could be used in resveratrol biosynthesis pathway evolution
Different final concentrations of p-coumaric acid and resveratrol were added into LB medium for 24-deep-well-plate fermentation at 30 °C.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Biosensor validated, ready for pathway evolution.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to evolve resveratrol production strain and identify improved variants.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Iterative mutagenesis and FACS sorting will enrich cells with higher resveratrol production.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Perform 16 mutagenesis passages, sort top 0.592‰ fluorescent cells (5,000 cells), retransform top 11 plasmids for verification.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Over 60% of sorted cells showed improved fluorescence; top 11 strains retransformed.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Analyze mutations in improved strains.

evidence.quote_or_span: After 16 consecutive mutagenesis passages, the cells with high fluorescence intensity were sorted from the saturated cultures by flow cytometry. A total of 5,000 cells were collected at a 0.592‰ sorting threshold. Over 60% of the cells sorted from the libraries exhibited improved fluorescence intensity compared with the starting strain
The collected cells were incubated on LB plates containing 900 mg/L p-coumaric acid.
Plasmids were extracted from the top 11 strains with highest fluorescence intensity, and retransformed into wild-type E. coli BL21 (DE3) to verify the increase in resveratrol production.
About 170 single colonies with darkest red were selected for 24-deep-well-plate fermentation at 30 °C.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Improved resveratrol production strains isolated, but mutations unknown.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to identify mutations responsible for improved phenotype.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Mutations may be in enzyme genes or plasmid backbone.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Sequence plasmids from top strains; construct test plasmids with individual mutations.
evolution_trajectory.observation: All improved plasmids contained ColE1 ori C148T and Δ5526-6717 deletion (mpETDuet); no mutations in enzyme genes; mpETDuet led to higher resveratrol production via increased mRNA levels (4-fold for STS, 2.9-fold for double mutant).
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Acknowledge limitations of the system.

evidence.quote_or_span: the relative mRNA level of STS in retransformed stains harboring mpETDuet was approximately 4 times higher than that in the wild type
the fluorescence intensity of mpETDuet was increased, by 28%
the double mutant (mpETDuet) was 2.9-fold mRNA level of the control
the deletion included the primosome assembly signal (pas) site, probably resulting in more durable Pol I replication and mutation in sequences located further from ori
Interestingly, two mutations, the ColE1 ori C148T mutation and the $\Delta$ 5526-6717 deletion, were identified in all plasmids; this double mutant was named mpETDuet.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: System demonstrated but with notable limitations.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Low mutation rate limits sequence space exploration; off-target genomic mutations occur; beneficial mutations may occur in non-coding regions.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: These limitations must be addressed for broader applicability.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Document limitations: low mutation rate, off-target genomic mutations requiring plasmid retransformation, and non-coding mutations masking protein improvements.
evolution_trajectory.observation: System has low mutation rate compared to advanced tools; genomic mutations occur; non-coding mutations can dominate.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Future work needed to overcome limitations.

evidence.quote_or_span: This limited mutational density had a negative impact on the exploration of sequence space and evolution speed.
The resulting undesirable mutations could occur in genes essential for cell survival and affect screening for beneficial mutations

The resulting mutants could bring desired phenotype changes unrelated to significant improvements in target protein properties, such as mutations occurring in the promoter, ribosome binding site and other regions associated with gene transcription and expression regulation, and thereby mask other variants carrying mutations within the protein-coding sequence

metrics: Percentage of cells with >20% activity increase
success_verification.final_conclusion: The ultrahigh-throughput screening-assisted in vivo directed evolution system successfully evolved α-amylase (N473Y, 48.3% improvement) and resveratrol pathway (1.7-fold improvement via plasmid backbone mutations), but limitations include low mutation rate, off-target genomic mutations, and non-coding mutations masking protein improvements.
limitations: Low mutation rate (6.77×10⁻⁸ per base per generation) limits sequence space exploration.
Off-target genomic mutations require plasmid retransformation between rounds.
Beneficial mutations may occur in non-coding regions rather than protein-coding sequences.

initial_request.research_problem: Classical directed evolution is labor- and time-intensive due to manual steps and limited screening throughput.
initial_request.known_context: Classical directed evolution methods rely on manual operation for rounds of stepwise processes, including DNA extraction, in vitro mutagenesis, transformation and selection, which are usually labour intensive and limit the efficiency, scale and depth of evolution.
constraints: Must enable continuous in vivo evolution
quantifiable_goals: Achieve >20% activity improvement in target enzymes
